def test_fail_job(self):
        fail = FailJob()
        self.assertRaises(RuntimeError, fail.run)
        # Check for retrials
        kube_api = HTTPClient(KubeConfig.from_file("~/.kube/config"))  # assumes minikube
        jobs = Job.objects(kube_api).filter(selector="luigi_task_id="
                                                     + fail.job_uuid)
        self.assertEqual(len(jobs.response["items"]), 1)
        job = Job(kube_api, jobs.response["items"][0])
        self.assertTrue("failed" in job.obj["status"])

### What it checks A non-exhaustive list of the various checks that are run: * Correct spelling (and provides possible corrections). * Use of non-inclusive/profane/offensive language. * Various prose-related checks, find the full list [here](https://github.com/btford/write-good#checks). ### Installation Visit [the installation page](https://github.com/apps/prosebot) and install the GitHub App on your repositories. That's all there is to it ❤️ ### Configuration There are currently three providers that the app uses to test your text. These can each be disabled, and are all enabled by default. To disable a provider, add a `.github/write-good.yml` file to your repository and set the provider to `false`: ```yaml # .github/write-good.yml writeGood: true alex: true spellchecker: true ``` ### Credits This Probot App is mostly a wrapper around existing open source libraries. The majority of the work is done by those, and they deserve a ton of thanks: * [`write-good`](https://github.com/btford/write-good) * [`alex`](https://github.com/get-alex/alex) * [`node-spellchecker`](https://github.com/atom/node-spellchecker) ### Future work * Using [Suggested Changes](https://blog.github.com/changelog/2018-10-16-suggested-changes/) to... suggest changes. * Work on multiple PRs at a time * Nicer annotations * More providers? import github.GithubObject


def encrypt(public_key: str, secret_value: str) -> str:
    """Encrypt a Unicode string using the public key."""
    public_key = public.PublicKey(public_key.encode("utf-8"), encoding.Base64Encoder())
    sealed_box = public.SealedBox(public_key)
    encrypted = sealed_box.encrypt(secret_value.encode("utf-8"))
    return b64encode(encrypted).decode("utf-8")

I am making this new pull request for clarity.
Change List
---------------
1. Add support to [Conditional Request](http://developer.github.com/guides/getting-started/#conditional-requests) API.
  * Add ```GithubObject.etag``` property
  * Add ```GithubObject.last_modified``` property
  * Modify ```Requester``` class to handle 304 response code
  * Modify ```Requester``` class to enable making conditional request
  * Add ```NotModifiedException``` class for 304 response code
  * Add ```GithubObject.update()``` method
  * Add a few private helper methods to make this work
2. Add methods for Data Persistence
  * Add ```GithubObject.save(file_fname)``` method
  * Add ```GithubObject.load(file_fname)``` class method
3. Test class for conditional request

Usage
---------------

import base64
import json

login = ""
password = ""
orgName = ""
repoName = "FailGithubApi"

def doRequest( verb, url, input ):
    input = json.dumps( input )
    cnx = httplib.HTTPSConnection( "api.github.com", strict = True )
    cnx.request( verb, url, input, { "Authorization" : "Basic " + base64.b64encode( login + ":" + password ).replace( '\n', '' ) } )
    response = cnx.getresponse()
    status = response.status
    output = response.read()
    cnx.close()
    print verb, url, input, "=>", status, output
    print
    if status < 200 or status >= 300:
        exit( 1 )
    return json.loads( output )

# Create a repo
doRequest( "POST", "/user/repos", { "name": repoName } )

# Create a blob, a tree, a commit and the master branch
b = doRequest(
    "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/git/blobs" % ( login, repoName ),
    { "content": "Content of the blob", "encoding": "latin1" }
)
t = doRequest(
    "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/git/trees" % ( login, repoName ),
    { "tree" : [ { "path": "foo.bar", "type": "blob", "mode": "100644", "sha": b["sha"] } ] }
)
c = doRequest(
    "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/git/commits" % ( login, repoName ),
    { "parents": [], "message": "Message of the commit", "tree": t["sha"] }
)
doRequest(
    "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/git/refs" % ( login, repoName ),
    { "ref": "refs/heads/master", "sha": c["sha"] }
)

# Fork the repo
doRequest( "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/forks?org=%s" % ( login, repoName, orgName ), None )

# Create a new blob => BOOM error 500
doRequest(
    "POST", "/repos/%s/%s/git/blobs" % ( orgName, repoName ),
    { "content": "Content of the new blob", "encoding": "latin1" }
)

Issue #855 

The class [IssueEvent](https://github.com/PyGithub/PyGithub/blob/master/github/IssueEvent.py) is missing a large number of attributes documented in the [API](https://developer.github.com/v3/issues/events/).

This is also commented about in #653 to a degree

27 of the tests 27 known event types have tests.

**Currently Tested using Issue #30**            
- [x] subscribed
- [x] assigned
- [x] referenced               
- [x] closed                   
- [x] labeled                  

**Currently Tested using Issue/PR #538**
- [x] merged
- [x] mentioned
- [x] review_requested

**Currently Tested using Issue/PR #857**
- [x] reopened
- [x] unassigned
- [x] unlabeled
- [x] renamed
- [x] base_ref_changed
- [x] head_ref_deleted 
- [x] head_ref_restored
- [x] milestoned
- [x] demilestoned
- [x] locked
- [x] unlocked
- [x] review_dismissed
- [x] review_reques

RUN echo "[req]\nprompt=no\ndistinguished_name = req_distinguished_name\n[req_distinguished_name]\ncountryName=AU" > /tmp/config
RUN openssl req -new -x509 -passin pass:kmeowx -config /tmp/config -days 365 -key ca-key.pem -sha256 -out ca.pem
RUN openssl genrsa -out server-key.pem -passout pass:fjxslq 4096
RUN openssl req -subj "/CN=docker" -sha256 -new -key server-key.pem -out server.csr
RUN echo subjectAltName = DNS:docker,DNS:localhost > extfile.cnf

If you use 2 factor authentication on your Github account, tests that require a login/password authentication will fail.
You can use `pytest Issue139.testCompletion --record --auth_with_token` to use the `oauth_token` field specified in `GithubCredentials.py` when recording a unit test interaction. Note that the `password = ""` (empty string is ok) must still be present in `GithubCredentials.py` to run the tests even when the `--auth_with_token` arg is used. (Also note that if you record your test data with `--auth_with_token` then you also need to be in token authentication mode when running the test. A simple alternative is to replace `token private_token_removed` with `Basic login_and_password_removed` in all your newly generated ReplayData files.)

Similarly, you can use `pytest Issue139.testCompletion --record --auth_with_jwt` to use the `jwt` field specified in `GithubCredentials.py` to access endpoints that require JWT.

I am making this new pull request for clarity.
Change List
---------------
1. Add support to [Conditional Request](http://developer.github.com/guides/getting-started/#conditional-requests) API.
  * Add ```GithubObject.etag``` property
  * Add ```GithubObject.last_modified``` property
  * Modify ```Requester``` class to handle 304 response code
  * Modify ```Requester``` class to enable making conditional request
  * Add ```NotModifiedException``` class for 304 response code
  * Add ```GithubObject.update()``` method
  * Add a few private helper methods to make this work
2. Add methods for Data Persistence
  * Add ```GithubObject.save(file_fname)``` method
  * Add ```GithubObject.load(file_fname)``` class method
3. Test class for conditional request

#### Configuring global authentication
1. Create a user in Jenkins which has, at a minimum, Job/Build permissions
2. Log in as that user (this is required even if you are a Jenkins admin user), then click on the user's name in the top right corner of the page
3. Click 'Configure,' then 'Show API Token...', and note/copy the User ID and API Token
4. In GitLab, when you create webhooks to trigger Jenkins jobs, use this format for the URL and do not enter anything for 'Secret Token': `https://USERID:APITOKEN@JENKINS_URL/project/YOUR_JOB`

i.e. to include openFrameworks headers in my own build, because all the includes look like:

#include "ofMesh.h"
#include "ofAppGlutWindow.h"
#include "ofSerial.h"

I have to add all these paths to my include paths:


  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/app
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/3d
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/communication
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/gl
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/graphics
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/utils
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/types
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/math
  ../Libraries/of_preRelease_v007_linux64/libs/openFrameworks/events



It is common to instead structure files like (in the case of openFrameworks):

oF/include/oF
oF/include/oF/app
oF/include/oF/3d
oF/include/oF/communication
oF/include/oF/gl
oF/include/oF/graphics
...etc...

and just write:
